content:
Phase 3 – Cloudinary Integration

We are now moving to Cloudinary integration.

IMPORTANT:
- Do NOT rebuild the app
- Do NOT change UI
- Do NOT change navigation
- Do NOT touch Firebase logic unless required for image wiring
- Only connect real Cloudinary image upload into the existing equipment creation/edit flow

Cloudinary setup is already prepared:
- cloud name is available
- unsigned upload preset is already created
- preset name: heavyar_unsigned
- folder: heavyar

Goal:
Replace any placeholder/local-only image handling with real Cloudinary upload.

Requirements:

1. Add real image upload support for equipment listings
- user picks image(s)
- image uploads to Cloudinary using unsigned preset
- returned image URL and publicId are stored in Firestore
- Firestore equipment document should save:
  images: [{ url, publicId }]

2. Use service abstraction
Create or update a dedicated service layer file such as:
- services/cloudinaryService.ts

3. Upload flow
- Use cloud name + unsigned upload preset
- No API secret in the app
- Handle upload loading state
- Handle upload failure state
- Prevent broken Firestore writes if upload fails

4. Create listing screen
- On create equipment:
  - upload selected image(s) first
  - then save equipment with final Cloudinary image URLs
- Keep the current UI style unchanged

5. Edit listing support
- If edit listing already exists, prepare it to support replacing or appending images cleanly
- Do not build a whole new edit UI if it does not already exist

6. Validation
- reject empty/invalid image upload states
- support jpg/png/webp if applicable
- keep code clean and reusable

7. Return only:
- files added/modified
- what is now fully working
- any remaining manual setup steps
